summary: Frontend UI/UX testing completed for elegant-sona pattern implementations. All 45+ test cases passed. Homepage, Explore, Recipe, Techniques, About, and Login pages all load correctly with proper CSS utilities applied.

issues: Slightly low contrast text (rgb(156,156,156)) on light background for 'Can't find what you're looking for?' - minor, still readable

critical_code_review_comments: All elegant-sona CSS patterns correctly implemented: fade-in-up animations (15 elements on homepage), section-divider (4 elements), scroll-container for mobile, recipe-card-hover (19 elements), nav-link-elegant (4 nav links)
var(--font-heading) correctly used across all pages - 54 elements on homepage, 10 on recipe page, 7 on about page
No hardcoded 'Cormorant Garamond' found in inline styles - all using CSS variable
Mobile horizontal scroll containers working: continents, dish types, curated recipes on HomePage
Font weight appears lighter as requested (font-light classes applied)
Footer renders with all expected links: Home, Explore, Menu Builder, Techniques, About, Contact, Editorial Policy, Privacy, Terms, Cookies, For AI
context_for_next_testing_agent: UI/UX improvements fully implemented. CSS utilities in index.css: fade-in-up (animation), scroll-container (mobile horizontal scroll), section-divider, nav-link-elegant, recipe-card-hover. All pages use var(--font-heading) for Cormorant Garamond font. Mobile viewport 375px shows horizontal scroll containers instead of grid. Desktop shows grid layouts. Explore page loads 161 recipes with pagination (24 initially).

notes: Minor design observation: The search helper text 'Can't find what you're looking for?' uses rgb(156,156,156) which is slightly light but still readable. All other contrast is good. Console shows 401 errors for /api/favorites/status which is expected for non-authenticated users.
